svg.text: • Routing protocols: OSPF, IS-IS run Dijkstra over link-state databases for IP routing.
• Maps / navigation: Google Maps, OSRM, Valhalla — Dijkstra + A* + contraction hierarchies.
• Game AI pathfinding: A* (Dijkstra + heuristic) on grid / nav-mesh.
• Network flow / scheduling: shortest augmenting paths in capacity-scaling max-flow.
• Telecom: minimum-cost path through capacitated optical networks.
• Robotics motion planning: grid + cell costs → shortest collision-free path.
• Currency arbitrage detection: Dijkstra on log-weights detects no negative cycle.
• Negative edges: silent wrong answers — detect at input or use Bellman-Ford.
• Float precision: tie-breaking on equal distances may flap path choice; canonicalize.
• Memory: lazy heap can hold O(E); on huge graphs prefer eager or external-memory variant.
• Dynamic graphs: any edge weight change invalidates results — use D* Lite or LPA*.
• Integer overflow: sum of weights can exceed INT_MAX — use 64-bit accumulators.
• Wrong PQ for graph density: log V overhead dominates on V² edges; switch to array.
Match the heap, weight type, and recompute cadence to the workload.
